# NEXT — acme-todo API hardening
<!-- generated by /ratchet-recommend from ratchet/BACKLOG.md (ratchet:v2), 2026-07-07 -->

> **The loop does not execute this file — you do.** Each wave below gives the exact command to
> paste. Waves marked `∥` sit in different lanes and run **at the same time**, each in its own
> worktree. Regenerate this plan on the integration branch after every merge.

Read from `master` with no unmerged `ratchet/*` branches and every lane closed — the routing
below is current. (Had a lane branch been ahead of `master`, its `done` rows would be invisible
from here, and this file would say so instead of pretending otherwise.)

## ▶ Start here

Wave 2 is two commands. Run them **concurrently**, in two terminals, in two worktrees:

```
git worktree add ../acme-api ratchet/api      # terminal 1 — a senior model, or a cheap one supervised
cd ../acme-api && claude
/ratchet-loop --lane api --only SEC-02 --verify fresh
```
```
git worktree add ../acme-ui ratchet/ui        # terminal 2 — a cheap model, unattended
cd ../acme-ui && claude --model sonnet
/ratchet-loop --lane ui --only UI-07 --verify inline
```

Neither agent can write the other's ledger, and their source scopes (`src/routes/**` vs
`web/src/**`) don't intersect — so the two branches merge without a conflict. While they run,
answer the UX-05 question below.

## Decisions needed from you

- **UX-05 — validation error wording:** users currently see raw zod paths (`body.title: Required`).
  Pick one: (a) generic "Please check your input" + field list, (b) per-field friendly messages
  (needs copy), or (c) keep raw errors for the API and fix display in the client. Answering this
  unblocks UX-05 — then unpark it on `master`, while the `api` lane is closed (the human write
  window): journal the decision in `lanes/api.md`, remove `[USER-DECISION]`, replace the
  provisional criterion with a real one, set status → `todo`.

## Waves

| # | ID | Lane | Route | Who | Verify | Why | Invocation |
|---|----|------|-------|-----|--------|-----|------------|
| 1 | UX-05 | api | DECIDE-FIRST | human answers **now**; executes later | — | `[USER-DECISION]`: wording not decided; criterion is provisional. Surfaced first so it isn't on the critical path | answer above, unpark, then `/ratchet-recommend` |
| 2a ∥ | SEC-02 | api | SUPERVISED | cheap model allowed, clean-room verified | fresh | Evidence mentions auth/ownership → *sensitive*; Spec names 1 file, no migration → *small* | `/ratchet-loop --lane api --only SEC-02 --verify fresh` |
| 2b ∥ | UI-07 | ui | AUTONOMOUS | cheap model (`claude --model sonnet`), unattended | inline | not sensitive; its one criterion is a runnable render test | `/ratchet-loop --lane ui --only UI-07 --verify inline` |
| 3 | PERF-03 | api | AUTONOMOUS | cheap model, unattended | inline | not sensitive; every AC is a runnable backticked command. Same lane as 2a → **after** it, in the same worktree, never beside it | `/ratchet-loop --lane api --only PERF-03 --verify inline` |
| — | — | api, ui | **MERGE** | human | re-run Global checks on the merge | both lanes closed → merge each into `master`. Merge commit or fast-forward **only**: squash and rebase-merge rewrite shas and orphan every `Commit:` cell in the ledgers. Lanes green apart can be red together, so the checks run again here | `git merge --no-ff ratchet/api && git merge --no-ff ratchet/ui && npm test` |
| 4 | UI-06 | core | AUTONOMOUS | cheap model, unattended | inline | **barrier wave**: the Spec adds `@tanstack/react-virtual`, so its diff touches `package.json` + the lockfile, which no named lane owns. Requires every other lane closed *and merged* — hence the merge row above | `/ratchet-loop --lane core --only UI-06 --verify inline` |
| 5 | OPS-04 | core | OPS | human/ops | — | `[OPS]`: needs staging server access to rotate the credential | rotate on staging and confirm the old password fails **yourself**; at unpark, rewrite that criterion as human-verified (Human transitions step 3) so every remaining criterion is loop-runnable; then remove `[OPS]`, journal it, status → `todo`, and run `/ratchet-loop --lane core --only OPS-04 --verify fresh` for the code half |

Every invocation carries **both** `--lane` and `--only`. `--lane` picks the state file and the
worktree; only `--only` satisfies the loop's high-stakes gate. A `--lane` command without
`--only` would park the lane's still-todo sensitive items instead of leaving them for their wave.

Waves 2a and 2b are the point of v2: two agents, one backlog, zero coordination cost. Wave 4 is
the price: anything that touches shared ground runs alone.

## Do NOT automate

- **The merges** — no executor merges lane branches, and squash/rebase-merge are banned.
- **OPS-04** — the rotation needs staging credentials no loop has; a cheap model "completing"
  this would mean marking a live leaked password handled when it isn't.
- **UX-05** — product copy is a human call; any model would be inventing your voice.
